discussion.p: The use of `template [: x :]` (introduced in LWG ) is incorrect: `template [: x :]` is a splice-expression (which can only designate a function template or variable template), and an unparenthesized splice-expression is not allowed as a template-argument per /6.

li.p: (1.1) — `template [: x :]`a template-argument that denotes what x represents if `is_template(x)` is `true`, otherwise
